answer_ref: Tree Traversal Retrieval uses a hierarchical approach, selecting top nodes at each level, while Collapsed Tree Retrieval flattens the tree into a single layer and retrieves nodes based on a token limit.
prediction: Tree Traversal Retrieval starts by encoding a query and then traversing the tree layer-by-layer, selecting the top-k most relevant nodes at each level based on cosine similarity to the query vector. It continues this process until reaching the leaf nodes, concatenating the text from all selected nodes to form the retrieved context. On the other hand, Collapsed Tree Retrieval flattens the multi-layered tree into a single layer, allowing it to consider all nodes simultaneously. It retrieves nodes based on cosine similarity until reaching a threshold number of tokens, then combines the retrieved context with the query to produce an answer using an LLM.
